file_insights.details: Multi-line reformatting of Command::new().args() call in start_server() function. Arguments expanded from single line to 6 lines (one per argument plus closing paren), improving readability and meeting formatter standards.

patterns_discovered.description: make check includes cargo fmt validation; code must comply with Rust formatting standards
patterns_discovered.context: Test integration code subject to same formatting rules as production code

patterns_discovered.description: When Command::new().args() contains 5+ arguments, format one argument per line for readability
patterns_discovered.evidence: port.to_string() conversion likely exceeded line length threshold

patterns_discovered.description: serve_smoke.rs is integration test for bop serve command; formatting compliance required for all test code
patterns_discovered.context: Tests subject to same quality gates as main codebase

gotchas_discovered.description: Running make check produced zero logic changes—only whitespace/formatting. Suggests code was already functionally correct.
gotchas_discovered.implication: Formatting gate is strict; developers must pre-format before commit or rely on CI

gotchas_discovered.description: Integration tests must pass cargo fmt same as production code. serve_smoke.rs formatting was not preemptively applied.
gotchas_discovered.context: bop-cli/tests directory is not exempt from formatting rules

recommendations.recommendation: Run cargo fmt before committing test code
recommendations.rationale: Integration tests in serve_smoke.rs should be pre-formatted to avoid CI friction
Clarify when to split arguments (appears to be ~80-88 chars based on port.to_string() trigger)

recommendations.recommendation: Consider pre-commit hook running cargo fmt
recommendations.rationale: Would catch formatting issues before make check stage, improving developer velocity
